title: Bean Instantiation Order When spring-boot-starter-web is Present
author: dcolazin

title: MongoDatabaseFactory initially used 'test' instead of database specified in spring.data.mongodb.uri
author: 3y45u
body:
## Spring Boot Version

4.1.0

## Java Version

17.0.12

## MongoDB Version

MongoDB Server 8.x
MongoDB Java Driver 5.8.0

## Description

While developing a Spring Boot application with Spring Data MongoDB, I observed unexpected behavior where the configured MongoDB URI was read correctly, but Spring Data initially used the `test` database instead of the database specified in the URI.

Configuration:

```properties
spring.data.mongodb.uri=mongodb://localhost:27017/jwt_db
```

I verified the following:

- `Environment#getProperty("spring.data.mongodb.uri")` returned:
  `mongodb://localhost:27017/jwt_db`

- Injected `MongoClient` behaved correctly:

```java
mongoClient.getDatabase("jwt_db").getName()
```

returned

```
jwt_db
```

However,

```java
mongoTemplate.getDb().getName()
```

and

```java
mongoDatabaseFactory.getMongoDatabase().getName()
```

both returned

```
test
```

The startup logs showed:

```
Using DB: test
URI      = mongodb://localhost:27017/jwt_db
Factory  = test
Template = test
```

At that point the application was operating on the `test` database.

## Additional Information

I confirmed that:

- there was no custom `MongoTemplate`
- there was no custom `MongoDatabaseFactory`
- there was no custom Mongo configuration class
- the MongoDB Java driver itself correctly accessed `jwt_db`

After deleting both the `test` and `jwt_db` databases from MongoDB and restarting the application, the issue disappeared.

Subsequent runs correctly reported:

```
Using DB: jwt_db
Factory  = jwt_db
Template = jwt_db
```

I have not yet been able to reproduce the problem consistently after that.

I'm opening this issue mainly to ask whether this behavior is expected or whether it could indicate an initialization or auto-configuration issue. If a reproducible sample is required, I can continue trying to reproduce it.

title: CloudFoundry reactive auto-configuration should not require a WebClient.Builder bean to be defined
author: matthew-js-porter
body:
<!--
Thanks for raising a Spring Boot issue. Please take the time to review the following
categories as some of them do not apply here.

🙅 "Please DO NOT Raise an Issue" Cases
- Question
STOP!! Please ask questions about how to use something, or to understand why something isn't
working as you expect it to, on Stack Overflow using the spring-boot tag.
- Security Vulnerability
STOP!! Please don't raise security vulnerabilities here. Head over to https://spring.io/security-policy to learn how to disclose them responsibly.
- Managed Dependency Upgrade
You DO NOT need to raise an issue for a managed dependency version upgrade as there's a semi-automatic process for checking managed dependencies for new versions before a release. BUT pull requests for upgrades that are more involved than just a version property change are still most welcome.
- With an Immediate Pull Request
An issue will be closed as a duplicate of the immediate pull request, so you don't have to raise an issue if you plan to create a pull request immediately.

🐞 Bug report (please don't include this emoji/text, just add your details)
Please provide details of the problem, including the version of Spring Boot that you
are using. If possible, please provide a test case or sample application that reproduces
the problem. This makes it much easier for us to diagnose the problem and to verify that
we have fixed it.

🎁 Enhancement (please don't include this emoji/text, just add your details)
Please start by describing the problem that you are trying to solve. There may already
be a solution, or there may be a way to solve it that you hadn't considered.


TIP: You can always edit your issue if it isn't formatted correctly.
     See https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown 
-->

For the servlet `CloudFoundryActuatorAutoConfiguration`, having a `RestClient.Builder` bean is optional and will fallback to a new Builder if not available. This makes having the `spring-boot-starter-restclient` an optional dependency when using `spring-boot-starter-actuator`

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/4c346884f379af3032d45041d8f620cc147b1a79/module/spring-boot-cloudfoundry/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/cloudfoundry/autoconfigure/actuate/endpoint/servlet/CloudFoundryActuatorAutoConfiguration.java#L103-L115

`CloudFoundryReactiveActuatorAutoConfiguration` should follow the same pattern. Today when using `spring-boot-starter-actuator` with a Webflux application you must also have `spring-boot-starter-webclient` (or create your own `WebClient.Builder` bean).

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/4c346884f379af3032d45041d8f620cc147b1a79/module/spring-boot-cloudfoundry/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/cloudfoundry/autoconfigure/actuate/endpoint/reactive/CloudFoundryReactiveActuatorAutoConfiguration.java#L108-L114

title: Version 3.5.16 shown as latest in GitHub Releases
author: filiphr
body: Not sure if this is on purpose or not, but the latest version shown in the GitHub Releases section is 3.5.16 The reason it is shown is because the release is done using the GitHub CLI with ``` gh release create ``` in https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/4c346884f379af3032d45041d8f620cc147b1a79/.github/actions/create-github-release/action.yml#L30C12-L30C30 The CLI has a `--latest` flag that can be set to `true` or `false`. However, in order to make it simpler for you I would advise using the GitHub REST API (https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/releases?apiVersion=2026-03-10#create-a-release) directly. This one has 3 options of the `make_latest` parameter: * `true` - the default and it is the same as `gh release create` * `false` * `legacy` - specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version. In my opinion this is the most correct one, as you want to see the latest as the version with the highest semantic version, independent on whether or not it was released before or after some earlier version

title: Add configurable timeout support to JmsHealthIndicator
author: marko-modsching-bison
body: The mentioned HealthIndicator uses a fixed time-interval of 5 seconds. In some cases it might be important to overwrite this value. For example when the service-broker has some pressure and can't respond within 5 seconds. Or the health-intervalls are configured to fire too often (many parallel pods with corresponding health checks). Thus the value should be configurable.

title: Nested JAR loader race condition with concurrent jar file access
author: janjanneck7777
body:
We observe intermittent failures at application startup using this combination of dependencies:
* Spring Boot 4.0.6
* MongoDB Java driver 5.8.0
* Elastic APM Java Agent 1.56.0

It manifests as a

`NoClassDefFoundError: com/mongodb/internal/connection/InternalStreamConnection` by all of the MongoDB monitor threads that initially try to establish a connection to our MongoDB Atlas cluster.

The same issue happens with Spring Boot 3.5.X and can be resolved by using the `LoaderImplementation.CLASSIC`. This workaround is no longer available starting with Boot 4. We also had success by
* unpacking the affected driver JARs using Gradle (`requiresUnpack("**/mongodb-driver-core-*.jar")`)
* disabling the APM agent entirely
* disabling the `mongodb` instrumentation of the APM agent

An (AI generated) reproducer can be found here: https://github.com/janjanneck7777/springboot-loader-race-repro. Run it using `./capture-diagnostics.sh`. On my machine the bug occurred in around 1 of 25 startups. 

We are aware that the APM agent declares support for Boot 4 as experimental. That being said: The same (or at least very similar) bug occurred using Boot 3 with the new loader. We have no issues when using the classic loader, suggesting there might be something that could be handled better in the new loader.

See also: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/issues/43290

I attached the output of the reproducer to this ticket.

title: New ConfigurationBeanNameGenerator causes unexpected behavior with Spring Boot
author: pip25
body:
Spring 7 has introduced the interface ConfigurationBeanNameGenerator that inherits from BeanNameGenerator. A generator that implements this interface has the ability to provide new default names for beans defined using Bean methods in configuration classes, while previous generators could only affect beans discovered via component scan. The new FullyQualifiedConfigurationBeanNameGenerator implements this interface in Spring 7.

This causes some problems for Spring Boot, however. Simply specifying
```java
@SpringBootApplication(nameGenerator = FullyQualifiedConfigurationBeanNameGenerator.class)
```
on the application class is insufficient, since this delegates the attribute value to ComponentScan and nowhere else, thus Bean method bean names will not be customized. ~~On the other hand, even more surprisingly, specifying this class in the SpringApplicationBuilder instead of the above annotation makes the name generator affect only the Bean method beans, and **not** the ones discovered via component scanning. So for the name generator to work correctly, it has to be specified **twice**.~~ EDIT: This is incorrect, see comment below.

To make things worse, tests do not use the SpringApplicationBuilder (and the application main method) by default, thus they only partially make use of the generator once again. (The useMainMethod attribute could theoretically serve as a workaround, though for me that crashes the test with an entirely different error regarding WebMvcConfigurationSupport, but that is outside of the scope of this bug report.)

What should Spring Boot do? 

- At the bare minimum, I think it should log a warning when it encounters a SpringBootApplication annotation that has a ConfigurationBeanNameGenerator set, but the same type of generator is not set in SpringApplication
- It could also automatically add such a generator to the SpringApplication, though I'm not sure if that would be a breaking change somehow. It would definitely help with making the tests work as they should without jumping through more hoops.

title: Harmonize Checkstyle configuration that validates license headers
author: snicoll
body: Forward port of issue #50921 to 4.1.x.

title: The new @RedisListener does not resolve property placeholders in topic attribute
author: yagyesha
body:
When using the new @RedisListener annotation, the topic attribute does not resolve Spring property placeholders.

For example:

```java
@RedisListener(
    id = "myListener",
    topic = "${app.my-channel}",
    consumes = "application/json"
)
public void handle(String message) {
    // ...
}
```

with:

```
app.my-channel=my-channel
```

results in the listener subscribing to the literal Redis channel:

```
${app.my-channel}
```

instead of:

```
my-channel
```

Is this an expected behavior? or is this a bug/missing feature?

title: Harmonize Checkstyle configuration that validates license headers
author: snicoll
body: We do check license using such a file for the main code, but the config for Gradle plugins is different.

title: Pin remaining GitHub Actions to SHAs for consistency
author: ypfaff
body:
While browsing the workflow files, I noticed that almost all external actions are pinned to a commit SHA with a version comment, e.g.:

```
uses: actions/checkout@9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0 # v7.0.0
```

Two actions are still referenced by a major-version tag:

- `actions/setup-java@v5` ([`prepare-gradle-build/action.yml#L37`](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/dc23ca59a560aa74743ed3e53428ad75efbf3e5d/.github/actions/prepare-gradle-build/action.yml#L37), [`publish-gradle-plugin/action.yml#L31`](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/dc23ca59a560aa74743ed3e53428ad75efbf3e5d/.github/actions/publish-gradle-plugin/action.yml#L31), [`verify.yml#L52`](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/dc23ca59a560aa74743ed3e53428ad75efbf3e5d/.github/workflows/verify.yml#L52))
- `actions/upload-artifact@v7` ([`verify.yml#L84`](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/dc23ca59a560aa74743ed3e53428ad75efbf3e5d/.github/workflows/verify.yml#L84), [`build-pull-request.yml#L21`](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/dc23ca59a560aa74743ed3e53428ad75efbf3e5d/.github/workflows/build-pull-request.yml#L21))

It looks like `actions/*` actions were switched to tags in August 2025 (see the polish of #46787), while with 9b281d52e51 (#50835) `actions/checkout` was pinned to a SHA again. Should these two be pinned to SHAs as well?

If so, I'd be happy to submit a PR. 🙂

title: spring-boot:build-image bind caches do not reuse BellSoft Liberica JRE layer
author: wind57
body:
here is the reproducer of the issue : https://github.com/wind57/spring-issue-reproduceer 

but the idea is that using : 

```
    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <image>
                        <name>docker.io/library/spring-boot-build-image-cache-repro:0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</name>
                        <buildWorkspace>
                            <bind>
                                <source>${pack.cache.root}/work</source>
                            </bind>
                        </buildWorkspace>
                        <buildCache>
                            <bind>
                                <source>${pack.cache.root}/build</source>
                            </bind>
                        </buildCache>
                        <launchCache>
                            <bind>
                                <source>${pack.cache.root}/launch</source>
                            </bind>
                        </launchCache>
                    </image>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
```

has no effect, the layers are re-downloaded. 

May be I miss-understood the documentation part here : https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/maven-plugin/build-image.html : 

> The caches and the build workspace can be configured to use bind mounts instead of named volumes, as shown in the following example:
